messages.text: <!channel> notes from today's discussion are <https://soyleiinnovations.slack.com/docs/T0128KNAQ02/F06M383FMAA|here> and also in the canvas for this channel

<!channel> We do need to have a "project sponsor/champion" for this. As the name implies, this is the person who carries the project over the finish line. I don't currently have the bandwidth to sponsor this project so someone else is going to need to step up. So far as I understand it, and <@U06F97BPQJU> is welcome to chime in here, the following is roughly what you need to know to be a sponsor:

1. The project sponsor works to move through the stage gate process. This may involve other people as needed, but ultimately is the responsibility of the project sponsor. 
2. The project sponsor will participate in Gate meetings. The main participants will be the Chair (Jim), the Lead Developer (me), and the sponsor. These will typically be monthly meetings in order to check the status of the project. 
3. The sponsor may, at any time, call a meeting to update the stage gate team. This would primarily be to pass through a gate. 
4. We do not skip stages. Gates must be opened before moving onto the next stage. 
I'm new to leading this process, so by all means folks are welcome to chime in if I've missed things or gotten anything wrong, but the primary action item from this is that someone needs to step up to be the sponsor for the polymer.
